summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that detects tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects, using SHA-256 digests and fallback filesystem walks.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA-256 digests for all project files; diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after to detect mutations
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation uses git ls-files and filesystem walk fallback

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard' to capture tracked, cached, and other files
evidence.why: Git-backed boundary detection captures all tracked and untracked files

evidence.summary: walkFiles() recursively scans filesystem excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Non-git boundary fallback walks filesystem while excluding common build/cache dirs

evidence.summary: Runtime calls buildBoundarySnapshot before/after agent execution and stores result in boundary.project_mutation field
evidence.why: Boundary audit is integrated into evidence dispatch runtime lifecycle

evidence.summary: Boundary violations add BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers to readiness and block PRD approval
evidence.why: Boundary violations are enforced as PRD readiness blockers

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' writes untracked file and expects blocked status
evidence.why: Evidence that git-backed boundary detects untracked file mutations

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs' writes .env file and expects violation
evidence.why: Evidence that gitignored mutations outside excluded dirs are detected

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' expects src/existing.txt modification detected
evidence.why: Evidence that non-git fallback detects modifications while excluding artifact root

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' expects blocked status when agent writes project file
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary violations block PRD readiness even when agents return success

evidence.summary: Triage evidence_policy is 'single_agent' for existing/hybrid projects, 'cross_check' for technical/high-cost risks
evidence.why: Evidence policy determines which agents are dispatched for boundary testing

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness requires explorer and verifier completion for single_agent policy
evidence.why: Readiness gates require evidence agents to complete before PRD approval

risks: Full-tool agent could bypass boundary if allowed_write_roots is misconfigured
Non-git walkFiles may miss files if excluded directories don't cover all build artifacts
SHA-256 collisions could theoretically mask mutations (cryptographically negligible)

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is complete with git and non-git coverage, proper exclusion logic, and PRD readiness enforcement. Tests confirm tracked, untracked, and gitignored mutation detection.

agent_results.claim: Verified: explorer evidence is complete, accurate, and supports PRD readiness for boundary audit implementation. All claims are backed by primary project sources with exact line references.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() and walkFiles() implementations exactly match explorer descriptions: git ls-files -co --exclude-standard for tracked/cached/other, recursive filesystem walk excluding standard build/cache dirs
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's line range accuracy and implementation details for boundary detection methods

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA-256 digests via hashFile(); diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after to detect added/modified/deleted files
evidence.why: Verifies core boundary audit mechanism matches explorer claim

evidence.summary: EvidenceDispatchRuntime.run() calls buildBoundarySnapshot at line 530 (pre-agent) and 554 (post-agent); stores mutation result in boundary.project_mutation
evidence.why: Confirms boundary audit integration into evidence dispatch lifecycle as claimed

evidence.summary: checkReadiness() adds BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker when boundary.project_mutation detected; sets blocked status and PRD readiness flag
evidence.why: Verifies PRD readiness enforcement mechanism matches explorer description

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' creates src/mutated.txt, expects blocked status and BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker code
evidence.why: Confirms boundary violations block PRD readiness even with successful agent results

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' writes untracked src/new-feature.ts, expects blocked and boundary_violation true
evidence.why: Verifies untracked file detection in git-backed projects

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs' writes .env, expects violation and blocked status
evidence.why: Confirms gitignored mutations are detected when outside excluded directories

evidence.summary: Non-git test writes src/existing.txt, expects boundary_violation true; artifact root /tmp/artifact-root is excluded from mutation detection
evidence.why: Verifies non-git fallback detects modifications while excluding artifact root

evidence.summary: Triage sets evidence_policy to 'single_agent' when reason_codes include EXISTING_PROJECT_FACTS or HYBRID_NEW_WORK_ON_EXISTING_SYSTEM
evidence.why: Confirms evidence policy routing logic matches explorer claim

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() blocks PRD approval when evidence_policy is 'single_agent' until explorer and verifier agents complete
evidence.why: Verifies readiness gates require evidence agent completion

assumptions: SHA-256 collision resistance is assumed to be cryptographically sufficient (standard assumption)
Excluded directories (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache) are assumed to cover all build artifacts in typical projects
risks: Full-tool agent with allowed_write_roots misconfiguration could theoretically bypass boundary if artifact root exclusion is too broad
Non-git walkFiles may miss mutations in non-standard build directories not in exclusion list
Edge case: file replaced with different content of identical SHA-256 hash (cryptographically negligible but theoretically possible)

result.notes: All explorer evidence verified against primary sources. Line references are accurate, implementation details match claims, and test coverage confirms tracked/untracked/gitignored mutation detection. Boundary audit is complete with proper git/non-git coverage, artifact root exclusion, and PRD readiness enforcement. Assumptions are reasonable and risks are documented.
